---
title: "LRP64: Do letter-sounds and vocabulary act as independent paths to word reading? (two-mediator decomposition)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The single-mediator [LRP59](../lrp-rli-med-059) (letter-sound only) and the route-composite
[LRP62](../lrp-rli-med-062) (letter-sound + blending) each collapse the question to one pathway.
LRP64 asks the **two-mediator** question directly: of the intervention's effect on
word reading $W$, how much runs through **letter-sound knowledge $L$**, how much
through **expressive vocabulary $E$**, and how much is direct/residual?

We extend the same g-formula machinery to a $\{L, E\}$ mediator block and report:

- the **joint indirect effect** through the block (the robust headline);
- a clearly-flagged **exploratory** path-specific split into $\text{NIE}_L$ and
  $\text{NIE}_E$.

## Phase 0 only — the design

The intervention is a clean randomised contrast **only in phase 0 (t1 → t2)**: group 1
receives the intervention, group 2 is the wait-list control; after t2 both arms are
treated. LRP64 uses the phase-0 transition only (`phase_mode="itt"`), $n \approx 53$.
Sign convention: $G = 2 - \text{group}$, so $G = 1$ is the intervention arm and
$G = 0$ the wait-list control (per `#117`); all effects are in the
**intervention-helps** (positive = benefit) direction.

## Causal structure

- **Treatment** $G$ (randomised). **Mediators** $L_{t2}$ and $E_{t2}$, each a
  Beta-Binomial leg conditioned on its own baseline. **Outcome** $Y = W_{t2}$
  (conditioned on $W_{t1}$). The outcome leg includes both $G\times L$ and $G\times E$
  interactions, so the decomposition admits exposure–mediator interaction.
- **Mediator–outcome confounders** $C$: receptive vocab $R$, age $A$, and the
  revised-DAG common causes hearing $HS$, phonological memory $RW$ and speech
  production $SP$ (entered by the missing-indicator method, #246), all taken at
  **baseline (t1)** so none is treatment-affected (the cross-world assumption).
  Expressive vocab is a *mediator* here, not a confounder. Whether $R$ remains a
  confounder once the revised DAG's edges are settled is a time-indexed
  d-separation question tracked in #264; it is **retained** here pending that.
- The natural (in)direct effects are computed by **counterfactual simulation from the
  posterior** (the g-formula), not from coefficients.

## Assumptions — read this first {#sec-assumptions}

This is a **decomposition under assumptions, not proof of a causal route.**

- Randomisation in phase 0 secures **no unmeasured confounding of $G\to Y$ and
  $G\to M$** for free.
- **No unmeasured mediator–outcome confounding** — the binding, unverifiable
  assumption and the main limitation. The revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, ID-2) names the specific threat:
  **latent general ability $GA$** confounds the mediator→outcome paths, so the
  NDE/NIE and the $\text{NIE}_L$/$\text{NIE}_E$ split are **adjusted associations,
  not point-identified**. We adjust for $A$, $R$, and the baselines, but $GA$ is
  latent and not directly observed — and, unlike the gain-/level-factor families,
  this between-child phase-0 mediation carries **no subject random intercept**, so it
  cannot even apply the partial latent-ability repair that intercept provides. Lead
  with the joint indirect effect; keep the $\text{NIE}_L$/$\text{NIE}_E$ split
  flagged exploratory.
- **A second, structural non-identification — independent of $GA$.** The
  latent-ability confounding above is not the only obstacle. Intervention **dose**
  (sessions attended — $\text{IS}$ in the DAG) is a **treatment-induced
  mediator–outcome confounder**: it is a descendant of the randomised group and
  points into both mediators ($L$, $E$) and word reading. So the natural
  direct/indirect effects are **not identified even if $GA$ were observed**, and —
  unlike ordinary confounding — the problem is **not repaired by measuring and
  adjusting dose**, because dose is itself a descendant of the exposure
  (VanderWeele, Vansteelandt & Robins 2014, DOI
  [10.1097/EDE.0000000000000034](https://doi.org/10.1097/EDE.0000000000000034)).
  The decomposition below is a **model-based g-formula decomposition under the
  stated (cross-world) assumptions, not an identified natural effect**. An
  **interventional** (rather than natural) mediation estimand — fitted for the
  letter-sound route as `lrp-rli-med-078` — escapes *this* obstacle, since it
  invokes no cross-world quantity. It is **not thereby identified**: it still
  assumes no unmeasured mediator–outcome confounding (Hejazi, Rudolph, van der Laan
  & Díaz 2022, assumption A5, DOI
  [10.1093/biostatistics/kxac002](https://doi.org/10.1093/biostatistics/kxac002)),
  which latent $GA$ violates here, and its positivity requirement and the missing
  temporal ordering remain binding. It is a weaker-assumption target, not a
  defensible one.
- **Contemporaneous measurement.** Both mediators and the outcome are at t2 — no
  temporal precedence within the window. Conditioning on baselines mitigates but does
  not remove this.
- **Sight-reading.** Children can read words without decoding, so $W$ does not reveal
  *how* a child read — only that the score moved.
- The two mediators are simulated as **conditionally independent given covariates** (no
  residual $L$–$E$ correlation is modelled), and the path-specific split is
  **ordering-dependent** (see below).
- Consistency and positivity are assumed.

::: {.callout-important title="Joint effect less order-sensitive; path split exploratory"}
With $n \approx 53$, expect **wide intervals**. The **joint indirect effect** (how much
flows through the $\{L, E\}$ block at all) is the **preferred descriptive
decomposition**: it is *less sensitive to mediator ordering* than the path split. That
is its only advantage — it is **not** a robust or identified quantity, because it
remains subject to both latent-general-ability confounding and exposure-induced $IS$
confounding. The $\text{NIE}_L$ / $\text{NIE}_E$ split is **exploratory**: it assumes a
mediator ordering (default $L$ before $E$) and the conditional-independence above, and
is an attribution under those assumptions — not a unique partition. Both are
model-based g-formula decompositions under the stated assumptions, **not identified
natural effects** (see the exposure-induced-confounder bullet above).
:::

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
